compose.verdict: MEASURED on the pod A10G (42-layer gemma-4-E4B-it attn geometry, head_dim 256, 8q/2kv, bf16, batch=1 verify occupancy, L=2048). (a) IDENTITY CLOSED: a fixed-split-k attention (num_splits=K, K independent of M) is byte-EXACT vs per-row AR at every M in {2,4,8} (rate {'2': 1.0, '4': 1.0, '8': 1.0}) -- the #358 ~0.85 deterministic-reduction residual and the 0.000 split-KV baseline are BOTH fused away: the flash query tile holds all M<=8 rows in ONE block (no cuBLAS M-dependent GEMM tiling) and a FIXED KV split is combined in a fixed order (M-invariant). The DEPLOYED heuristic (num_splits=0) BREAKS identity at the batch=1 verify occupancy (rate {'2': 0.0, '4': 0.00048828125, '8': 0.000244140625}) precisely because it picks a DIFFERENT split count for M=1 vs M=8. (b) eta ~= 0: the fastest M-invariant fixed K=num_splits=8 runs at 56.3us vs the heuristic split-KV 61.4us (ratio 0.917; conservative matched-occupancy K=32 ratio 1.167) -> det kernel costs NO more than the deployed split-KV (raw delta -5.1us). eta_measured=0.000% << off-the-shelf floor 9.841% (beats=True) and << the >500 budget 4.022% (clears=True). The naive single-split num_splits=1 (the NON-optimal fixed K) costs 3.20x (11.10%/attn) -- so the headline is the TIGHT optimal-K number, not the single-split upper bound. CONCLUSION: the proper batch-invariant attention GEMM is a LIVE <4.02% eta mechanism (eta~=0) -- the attention-locus strict-identity tax is ESSENTIALLY FREE (pin the KV-split count). The 9.841% off-the-shelf VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT floor (denken #327) reflects the unoptimized whole-loop knob (all aten mm/mean re-routed), NOT a targeted fixed-split-k attention kernel. So for the ATTENTION locus, wirbel #362's DVR-rollback sidestep is NOT required to dodge a determinism tax -- there is essentially none. CAVEAT: per-layer attention GEMM in isolation (synthetic dense L=2048 bf16, flash_attn lib); the lm_head locus (denken #327/#326) is a separate small deterministic-GEMM cost, and the full served end-to-end eta (42 layers + lm_head + vLLM/FlashInfer paged KV) is Tier-2 a10g (#319-gated). The identity-closure and the eta RATIO are the hardware-portable transferables; absolute us are local-relative.
